LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC. DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT CHEVRON IS RESPONSIBLE FOR HIS DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT IDENTIFY THE ALLEGEDLY INJURY-PRODUCING TOXIN, EXPOSURE TO SAMPLE MANUFACTURED OR SUPPLIED BY CHEVRON, OR ALLEGE HOW THE TOXIN ENTERED HIS BODY. (SEE BOCKRATH VS. ALDRICH CHEMICAL CO. (1999) 21 CAL. 4TH 71.) FRAUD IS NOT ALLEGED WITH SPECIFICITY. NO FACTS ESTABLISH EXTREME OR OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT BY CHEVRON TO SUPPORT EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.(302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ATLANTIC RICHFIELD COMPANY'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT CHEVRON USA INC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL 60 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST IDENTIFY THE INJURY-PRODUCING TOXIN AND LINK SUCH TOXIN TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD HIS FRAUD CLAIM WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY AND ALLEGE THE ELEMENTS OF EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT TO SUPPORT CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: William Rouse, Esq. Plaintiff appearing in Pro Per and opposing party; Colleen O'Brien, Esq. of Steptoe & Johnson LLP (213) 439-9425 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Late opposition considered. Plaintiff fails to allege that Exxon is responsible for his damages. Plaintiff does not identify the allegedly injury-producing toxin, exposure to a sample manufactured or supplied by Exxon, or allege how the toxin entered his body. See Bockrath vs. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 71.) Fraud is not alleged with specificity. No facts establish extreme or outrageous conduct by Exxon to support emotional distress claim. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Apr-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC. DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: William Rouse, Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing and opposing party; Lee Kaster, Esq. of Filice Brown LLP (510) 444-3131 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that Chevron is responsible for his damages. Plaintiff does not identify the allegedly injury-producing toxin, exposure to sample manufactured or supplied by Chevron, or allege how the toxin entered his body. (See Bockrath vs. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 71.) Fraud is not alleged with specificity. No facts establish extreme or outrageous conduct by Chevron to support emotional distress claim. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC. DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Plaintiff William Rouse, (415) 216-8770 appearing in Pro Per and opposing party; Lee L. Kaster, Esq. of Filice Brown Eassa & McLeodd LLP, (510) 444-3131 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with thirty days leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state facts regarding exposure to defendant's alleged injury-producing toxin, or to sufficiently identify the injury-producing toxin. (See Bockrath vs. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71.) Insufficient facts alleged regarding third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar May-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ATLANTIC RICHFIELD COMPANY'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: William Rouse, 216-8770 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Peter A. Strotz, Esq. of Filice Brown Eassa & McLeod LLP 510/444-3131 for deft Chevron U.S.A. Inc.; Benjamin D. Ammerman, Esq. of Tropio & Morlan 818/883-4000 for deft & moving party Atlantic Richfield Company (appearing via CourtCall).
RULING: Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL 60 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST IDENTIFY THE INJURY-PRODUCING TOXIN AND LINK SUCH TOXIN TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD HIS FRAUD CLAIM WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY AND ALLEGE THE ELEMENTS OF EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT TO SUPPORT CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (302/CWW/JW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
